Storybook

Guides

Learn how to install, configure, and customize the Storyhouse design system.

Getting Started

Install the design system packages and configure them in your project. Covers npm setup, TypeScript configuration, and basic usage patterns.

Read guide →

Design Tokens

Understanding colors, typography, spacing, and shadow tokens. Available as CSS custom properties and JavaScript exports.

Browse tokens →

Theming Guide

Customize the design system to match your brand. Learn about CSS variable overrides, dark mode, and token customization.

Read guide →

Utilities

Learn about the cn() class merge utility, TypeScript helpers, and Tailwind integration utilities shipped with Storyhouse.

Read guide →

Common Patterns

Reusable UI patterns built with Storyhouse — form layouts, navigation, data display cards, responsive grids, and user menus.

View patterns →

Contributing

Guidelines for contributing to Storyhouse — component structure, coding standards, Storybook stories, testing, and PR process.

Read guide →

Migration Guide

Upgrade between Storyhouse versions with confidence. Covers breaking changes, deprecations, API notes, and the upgrade checklist.

Read guide →
ESC
Type to search components, guides, tokens, and more…

Storyhouse Design System

Built for scale, precision, and exceptional developer experience.
